steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. Sentence 1 establishes a fact: the Eloi are not real; they are fictional. Sentence 2 says they were prime candidates for a book of imaginary beings. The blank must express the logical connection: sentence 2 is a consequence that follows directly from sentence 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Granted,: introduces a concession, acknowledging something that might seem to work against the writer's point. No concession is being made here. ✗
  • Choice B — In reality,: introduces a contrasting truth that corrects a misconception. There is no misconception to correct. ✗
  • Choice C — As such,: introduces a logical consequence that follows from what was just stated. Because the Eloi are fictional, it follows that they would be candidates for a compendium of imaginary beings. ✓
  • Choice D — For example,: introduces a specific illustration of a general claim. Sentence 1 makes no general claim that sentence 2 could exemplify. ✗
C is correct.
